TRANSCRIPT
Welcome to Operation Quick Start. This course, this program, is all about getting
you started fast, getting you an online business fast, getting you success fast. And just as
important, having that be sustainable, having it be something that is going to last, that's
built to last, that will grow over time. It's about you having a business where you see early
progress that encourages you, and then you can build on that progress. And the thing I
want you to understand is that this is completely possible.
Sometimes people look at the idea, or they think of the idea of an online business,
and it seems like a fun pipe dream that all the freedom that entails, just wow. Wouldn't
that be cool? But it's not really possible. Well, I'm here to tell you that it is possible
because I've been living this now for a very long time, and I've helped a lot of other people
create businesses, build businesses, and transform their lives.
If you look around, it's just obvious there's lots and lots of people that have online
businesses and that are having success. And you can have success too, if you follow what I
lay out here, if you follow Operation Quick Start. It's a formula that you can follow, but
there are certain things you have to focus on, and there are certain things you have to
forget about, at least initially, and that's what this course is all about.
I'm Jeff Walker, and I started my first online business way back in 1996. I've been
in business since then. And I had an initial goal for my business, and I hit that goal, and
then I grew and grew, and eventually grew to 10 times that goal. And then it kept on
growing, and it became 10 times that goal. And then it kept growing, and became 10 times
that goal. And my business is now 1000 times, 1000 times bigger than my initial goal for
my business. It's absolutely ridiculous, but it shows you what's possible.
I started my business from absolute desperation. I was a stay-at-home dad. I
needed a change in my life. I needed to help support my family. We were just scraping by,
paycheck to paycheck, barely covering the bills. And I was desperate for a change. So I'd
been studying the stock market for years before, so I actually decided to start publishing
about the stock market. I had no credentials. I had no letters after my name. I wasn't a
stock broker, or a commodity trading advisor, or a registered investment advisor, or
anything like that. I had no official training, but I started publishing.
I published a newsletter, a free newsletter about the stock market. Back then, I
couldn't afford a domain name. I couldn't afford web hosting. I used a free web host, put
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

up a really basic site, and started sending an email - an email newsletter. And over time,
people started to subscribe to that newsletter, and that was a real big key. And I'll be
talking about that in just a few minutes. And eventually, I've got several hundred people
getting that newsletter, and I decided that I would create a paid newsletter, a paid version
of that newsletter. And then I did that, and I launched it. And I would later go on to
become famous for my launches, and famous for teaching people how to do launches,
product launches, service launches.
But that was the first launch. That brought in $1650 and that changed my life. It
wasn't so much the money, although the money was huge to me at the time. But what was
incredible was this idea that people would actually pay me for my products, that I could
create something of value, and people would pay me for it. And that changed everything.
And then it just grew, and grew, and grew, and grew from there. The point where my best
launch now exceeded five million dollars. It was 5.1 million dollars I did, in five days’
times.
I've done all kinds of other crazy things. I've had many days where I've generated a
million dollars in sales. I had a single hour that generated a million dollars in sales. I
don't tell you any of this to brag. It's just that the most important step is that first
step, and if I hadn't done that launch of $1650, then I never would have gotten to where
I'm at.
So this is all about getting you to take those initial actions that are going to set you
up for nonstop growth. I've learned a lot in the last 20 years, and this course is all about
helping you take that first step, and having early success, and get going quickly. And it's
not just that I've had the success, it's I've coached now tens of thousands of people. Some
of them have started from zero. And actually, some of them have million and multi-
million dollar businesses.
But a lot of people never went anywhere, people that had the same access to the
same training, the same coaching, the same information. So some have multi-million
dollar businesses, but some of them ... It just never happened for them. Or they had really
short-term success, and then they faded away and bounced off to something else.
So what I've done in this course is I've tried to focus on what I see are the key
differences between those, the people that succeed and the people that don't succeed. And
what I want to present to you here is how to get that quick start that sets you up for the
long-term success.

So this is what I've learned about getting started. You want to get started fast
because too many people spend too much time learning, and learning, and learning, and
learning, and planning, and planning, and planning, and thinking, and thinking, and
thinking, and looking for perfection, and just on and on, perfection, perfection,
perfection, but they never actually get started. So you need to start. You need to actually
get into movement, into motion, because momentum brings more momentum. And life is
easier when you have momentum.
But just as important as that quick start is stamina, business stamina, marketing
stamina. And I think this is why I've had success and I've been able to sustain success
over time. You know, I've really ... As I look back on it, I stumbled onto a few things early
on. And what I did was I created incremental success. So I could see this incremental
getting better and better, and seeing it start to grow. Even though maybe there weren't
massive dollars, I was seeing these steps, these things that were growing.
And incremental success, in a large part, comes down to how you measure it, how
you measure your initial success. Sometimes people, they look at progress, and they think
about the wrong things. I want you to focus on the right things. They look at the wrong
things, which is getting a logo put together, or opening up bank accounts, or getting a
fancy design done on your blog or your website, or getting a lot of people to like you on
social media. But what I realized early on was that it was all about gathering, well now I
call it a tribe or a community, but back then it was subscribers, email subscribers.
And for me, it was all about getting people to subscribe to my newsletter. That was
it. And I knew as long as I was getting more subscribers and I was growing my email list,
that good things were going to happen. And I, for some reason, got that right from the
very, very beginning. The very first day, I understood that. And I've always focused on
that. And I would suggest there's lots of different businesses, lots of different business
models, but I would suggest that's a good place for you to start, a very good place to start.
I knew subscribers were valuable, so I focused on getting more subscribers every
single day. Initially, I was not getting any subscribers. And then as I grew, maybe every
third or fourth day, I would get a single subscriber. And then it got to point where I'd get a
new subscriber every single day, and that was awesome. And then I was getting like three
or four a day. And I kept on working to grow that number, and grow it, and grow it. And
then I was getting sixteen a day, and then 23 a day. And the thing is if you were to get 34
per day, that's 1000 subscribers per month. Thirty-four a day is 1000 subscribers a
month, 12,000 subscribers a year.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

I actually built a newsletter over the span, this is my stock market newsletter, over
the span of, I don't know, about eight years. And I eventually got that list, that subscriber
list, up to about 80,000 people. And that might sound like an incredible number, an
insane number, but really that was a matter of getting 30 to 35 people a day, day after
day, month after month, year after year. And then that's how you end up with 80,000
subscribers. It's amazing.
People often way overestimate what they can get done in a very short people of
time, but they underestimate what you can get done over a lengthy time, over a long
period of time. One way to think about this is ... A really good benchmark ... I don't know
if it's a good benchmark, but one to have in your head, an average benchmark, is that in
most markets, or in many markets ... Let's just say this is an average. It's not a guarantee.
Some markets are better. Some are worse.
But a good benchmark is that you can generate one dollar per month per
subscriber. So if you have say 10,000 subscribers, that's $10,000 per month every month,
month after month. So if you start to think of it that way, if you get, let's just say that you
get 10 people a day to join your list, that's 300 people a month. Within four months,
you're at 1200 people. That's $1200 a month, and you're starting to make money. Not
bad. Not bad.
And these numbers, I'll tell you for me in my business, it's way higher than a dollar
per month per subscriber, way higher. And if you're in my world and start to pay attention
to my marketing and my training, then your numbers will probably higher than that
dollar per month per subscriber. But it's a good benchmark.
So early on, as I watched my subscribers grow, I knew I was making progress. That
is what kept me moving forward. I knew I was making progress. You might want to do the
same. Social media followers don't count. Email subscribers count. And that's
what's going to grow your following, grow your community, grow your power.
Now I have a very large email list, and it's almost like I can generate money on
demand. If something comes up and I need money, I can put together a promotion, send
an email, and boom, I've got money.
So in this Operation Quick Start, I want to get you started fast and I want to get you
started in a sustainable manner. And part of that, a big part, is just understanding what is
moving you forward. And I would just suggest that building your following,
building your tribe, building your email subscribers is the thing. And
